description: Nov–Feb polar night reduces solar to <2% of summer capacity. Consistent across all 10 years of data.
mitigation: Nuclear baseload covers the Nov–Feb gap entirely. No renewable augmentation possible.

description: Temperatures below -30°C cause blade icing and mechanical failure in standard turbines. Occurs 3-4 months/year at 68N.
mitigation: Arctic-rated cold-weather turbine packages; reactor provides backup during turbine maintenance.

description: Arctic communities rely on annual sealift or air freight for diesel. A single missed sealift = energy emergency.
mitigation: Micro-reactor eliminates diesel dependency entirely for baseload power generation.

description: Scheduled and unscheduled maintenance requires reactor shutdown. Community loses primary power source.
mitigation: Schedule maintenance in summer (June–Aug) when renewables can partially compensate. Battery buffer (2hr).

description: Climate change is accelerating permafrost thaw at Arctic sites. Foundation instability can compromise reactor siting.
mitigation: Thermosyphon foundation system; mandatory geotechnical survey pre-construction; CNSC siting requirements.

description: Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission licensing for novel reactor designs can take 3-7 years without pre-engagement.
mitigation: Early pre-licensing vendor design review (VDR) with CNSC. Engage Indigenous consultation process early.

description: Nuclear technology faces public perception challenges. Arctic Indigenous communities have sovereignty concerns.
mitigation: Community co-ownership model; transparent public communications; benefit-sharing agreements.
